The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from {{ill|Mount Tsukuba|ja|筑波山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakae, Chiba]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[en:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]

{{Infobox Kofun of Japan |name=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (龍角寺岩屋古墳) |image=[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance |location={{ill|Sakae, Chiba|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture, Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|49|16|N|140|16|38|E|region:JP-12_scale:20000}} |shape=Square Kofun |dimensions=78 m on each side, 13.2 m in height |construction_period=Early–mid 7th century |burial goods=Haniwa in the shape of waterfowl |designation=National Historic Site (1941) |Burial Facility=Two horizontal stone chambers |map={{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=49|lat_sec=16|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=38|label=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Location in Chiba Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun''' (龍角寺岩屋古墳) is a square Kofun (方墳) located in {{ill|Sakae|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is designated as No. 105 in the {{ill|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|ja|龍角寺古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which comprises 115 Kofuns. Together with the Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, it is recognized as a [[Historic Site of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:100501 R Iwaya kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Aerial view of Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (2010) from the Geospatial Information Authority of Japan aerial photograph]] The kofun is located on a terrace about 30 meters above sea level, on the northern edge of {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a marsh in Chiba Prefecture. Scholars estimate its construction to the early–mid 7th century, during the late Kofun period.Kurita (2005), p. 158; Shiraishi (2007), p. 178; Ozawa (2008), p. 336. However, the lack of unearthed artifacts from the site has led to debates about its precise construction period, with estimations based primarily on the architectural features of the stone chambers.Ōtsuka (2009), p. 39. The neighboring {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is thought to have been built slightly earlier, around the early 7th century. The mound has three stepped tiers, with each side measuring 78 meters and a height of 13.2 meters. Surrounding it is a 3-meter-wide moat and embankment. With its large size, the kofun surpasses contemporary square mounds such as the {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (tomb of Emperor Yōmei) and {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (tomb of Empress Suiko). Nationally, it Ranks as the second-largest square Kofun after {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, built in the early 5th century in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Shiraishi (2007), p. 159. The southern face of the mound features two horizontal stone chambers positioned 10 meters apart. The western chamber measures 4.23 meters in length, with a rear wall width of 1.68 meters and a height of 2.14 meters. The eastern chamber, slightly larger, has partially collapsed. Both chambers were constructed with sandstone from the {{ill|Kinoshita Formation|ja|下総層群木下層|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for its abundant fossilized shells.[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n431-019.html Kinoshita Formation] (Chiba Prefecture). File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Southwest corner of the mound (stone chamber opening visible in the background) File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Northwest corner and embankment File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the eastern and western chambers File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance to the eastern chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-4.jpg|Entrance to the western chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Interior of the western chamber == Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster == Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun is part of the larger Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, which contains 114 known Kofuns. Located on the {{ill|Shimōsa Plateau|ja|下総台地|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in northeastern Chiba, construction in the cluster is believed to have started in the 6th century with smaller {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and round Kofuns (円墳). By the early 7th century, larger keyhole-shaped mounds such as the Sengenyama Kofun were built, followed by square mounds like the Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun. == Design and Burial Facilities == The mound measures 78 meters per side and rises 13.2 meters high. It features three stepped tiers with a moat surrounding three sides and an embankment beyond. In 2008, surveyors identified a ramp on the southern side leading to the mound.Jinno (2009), p. 15. Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from {{ill|Mount Tsukuba|ja|筑波山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakae, Chiba]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[en:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]

Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from {{ill|Mount Tsukuba|ja|筑波山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Tsukuba|qq=}} was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakae, Chiba]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[en:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun of Japan |name=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (龍角寺岩屋古墳) |image=[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance |location={{ill|Sakae, Chiba|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae, Chiba|qq=}}, Chiba Prefecture, Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|49|16|N|140|16|38|E|region:JP-12_scale:20000}} |shape=Square Kofun |dimensions=78 m on each side, 13.2 m in height |construction_period=Early–mid 7th century |burial goods=Haniwa in the shape of waterfowl |designation=National Historic Site (1941) |Burial Facility=Two horizontal stone chambers |map={{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=49|lat_sec=16|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=38|label=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Location in Chiba Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun''' (龍角寺岩屋古墳) is a square Kofun (方墳) located in {{ill|Sakae|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae|qq=}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is designated as No. 105 in the {{ill|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|ja|龍角寺古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which comprises 115 Kofuns. Together with the Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, it is recognized as a [[Historic Site of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:100501 R Iwaya kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Aerial view of Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (2010) from the Geospatial Information Authority of Japan aerial photograph]] The kofun is located on a terrace about 30 meters above sea level, on the northern edge of {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}, a marsh in Chiba Prefecture. Scholars estimate its construction to the early–mid 7th century, during the late Kofun period.Kurita (2005), p. 158; Shiraishi (2007), p. 178; Ozawa (2008), p. 336. However, the lack of unearthed artifacts from the site has led to debates about its precise construction period, with estimations based primarily on the architectural features of the stone chambers.Ōtsuka (2009), p. 39. The neighboring {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} is thought to have been built slightly earlier, around the early 7th century. The mound has three stepped tiers, with each side measuring 78 meters and a height of 13.2 meters. Surrounding it is a 3-meter-wide moat and embankment. With its large size, the kofun surpasses contemporary square mounds such as the {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Emperor Yōmei) and {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Empress Suiko). Nationally, it Ranks as the second-largest square Kofun after {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}}, built in the early 5th century in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara, Nara|qq=}}.Shiraishi (2007), p. 159. The southern face of the mound features two horizontal stone chambers positioned 10 meters apart. The western chamber measures 4.23 meters in length, with a rear wall width of 1.68 meters and a height of 2.14 meters. The eastern chamber, slightly larger, has partially collapsed. Both chambers were constructed with sandstone from the {{ill|Kinoshita Formation|ja|下総層群木下層|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita Formation|qq=}}, known for its abundant fossilized shells.[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n431-019.html Kinoshita Formation] (Chiba Prefecture). File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Southwest corner of the mound (stone chamber opening visible in the background) File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Northwest corner and embankment File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the eastern and western chambers File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance to the eastern chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-4.jpg|Entrance to the western chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Interior of the western chamber == Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster == Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun is part of the larger Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, which contains 114 known Kofuns. Located on the {{ill|Shimōsa Plateau|ja|下総台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimōsa Plateau|qq=}} in northeastern Chiba, construction in the cluster is believed to have started in the 6th century with smaller {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and round Kofuns (円墳). By the early 7th century, larger keyhole-shaped mounds such as the Sengenyama Kofun were built, followed by square mounds like the Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun. == Design and Burial Facilities == The mound measures 78 meters per side and rises 13.2 meters high. It features three stepped tiers with a moat surrounding three sides and an embankment beyond. In 2008, surveyors identified a ramp on the southern side leading to the mound.Jinno (2009), p. 15. Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from {{ill|Mount Tsukuba|ja|筑波山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Tsukuba|qq=}} was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakae, Chiba]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[en:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]

File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Southwest corner of the mound (stone chamber opening visible in the background) File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Northwest corner and embankment File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the eastern and western chambers File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance to the eastern chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-4.jpg|Entrance to the western chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Interior of the western chamber == Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster == Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun is part of the larger Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, which contains 114 known Kofuns. Located on the [[:en:Shimōsa Plateau|Shimōsa Plateau]] in northeastern Chiba, construction in the cluster is believed to have started in the 6th century with smaller [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and round Kofuns (円墳). By the early 7th century, larger keyhole-shaped mounds such as the Sengenyama Kofun were built, followed by square mounds like the Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun. == Design and Burial Facilities == The mound measures 78 meters per side and rises 13.2 meters high. It features three stepped tiers with a moat surrounding three sides and an embankment beyond. In 2008, surveyors identified a ramp on the southern side leading to the mound.Jinno (2009), p. 15. Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from [[:en:Mount Tsukuba|Mount Tsukuba]] was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakae, Chiba]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[en:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Infobox Kofun" to "Infobox ancient site"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site of Japan |name=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (龍角寺岩屋古墳) |image=[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance |location={{ill|Sakae, Chiba|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae, Chiba|qq=}}, Chiba Prefecture, Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|49|16|N|140|16|38|E|region:JP-12_scale:20000}} |shape=Square Kofun |dimensions=78 m on each side, 13.2 m in height |construction_period=Early–mid 7th century |burial goods=Haniwa in the shape of waterfowl |designation=National Historic Site (1941) |Burial Facility=Two horizontal stone chambers |map={{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=49|lat_sec=16|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=38|label=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Location in Chiba Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun''' (龍角寺岩屋古墳) is a square Kofun (方墳) located in {{ill|Sakae|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae|qq=}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is designated as No. 105 in the [[:en:Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster]], which comprises 115 Kofuns. Together with the Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, it is recognized as a [[Historic Site of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:100501 R Iwaya kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Aerial view of Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (2010) from the Geospatial Information Authority of Japan aerial photograph]] The kofun is located on a terrace about 30 meters above sea level, on the northern edge of {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}, a marsh in Chiba Prefecture. Scholars estimate its construction to the early–mid 7th century, during the late Kofun period.Kurita (2005), p. 158; Shiraishi (2007), p. 178; Ozawa (2008), p. 336. However, the lack of unearthed artifacts from the site has led to debates about its precise construction period, with estimations based primarily on the architectural features of the stone chambers.Ōtsuka (2009), p. 39. The neighboring {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} is thought to have been built slightly earlier, around the early 7th century. The mound has three stepped tiers, with each side measuring 78 meters and a height of 13.2 meters. Surrounding it is a 3-meter-wide moat and embankment. With its large size, the kofun surpasses contemporary square mounds such as the {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Emperor Yōmei) and {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Empress Suiko). Nationally, it Ranks as the second-largest square Kofun after {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}}, built in the early 5th century in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]].Shiraishi (2007), p. 159. The southern face of the mound features two horizontal stone chambers positioned 10 meters apart. The western chamber measures 4.23 meters in length, with a rear wall width of 1.68 meters and a height of 2.14 meters. The eastern chamber, slightly larger, has partially collapsed. Both chambers were constructed with sandstone from the {{ill|Kinoshita Formation|ja|下総層群木下層|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita Formation|qq=}}, known for its abundant fossilized shells.[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n431-019.html Kinoshita Formation] (Chiba Prefecture). File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Southwest corner of the mound (stone chamber opening visible in the background) File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Northwest corner and embankment File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the eastern and western chambers File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance to the eastern chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-4.jpg|Entrance to the western chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Interior of the western chamber == Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster == Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun is part of the larger Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, which contains 114 known Kofuns. Located on the [[:en:Shimōsa Plateau|Shimōsa Plateau]] in northeastern Chiba, construction in the cluster is believed to have started in the 6th century with smaller [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and round Kofuns (円墳). By the early 7th century, larger keyhole-shaped mounds such as the Sengenyama Kofun were built, followed by square mounds like the Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun. == Design and Burial Facilities == The mound measures 78 meters per side and rises 13.2 meters high. It features three stepped tiers with a moat surrounding three sides and an embankment beyond. In 2008, surveyors identified a ramp on the southern side leading to the mound.Jinno (2009), p. 15. Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from [[:en:Mount Tsukuba|Mount Tsukuba]] was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakae, Chiba]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[en:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]

Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from [[:en:Mount Tsukuba|Mount Tsukuba]] was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakae, Chiba]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[en:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:千葉県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:千葉県の古墳
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site of Japan |name=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (龍角寺岩屋古墳) |image=[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance |location={{ill|Sakae, Chiba|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae, Chiba|qq=}}, Chiba Prefecture, Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|49|16|N|140|16|38|E|region:JP-12_scale:20000}} |shape=Square Kofun |dimensions=78 m on each side, 13.2 m in height |construction_period=Early–mid 7th century |burial goods=Haniwa in the shape of waterfowl |designation=National Historic Site (1941) |Burial Facility=Two horizontal stone chambers |map={{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=49|lat_sec=16|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=38|label=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Location in Chiba Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun''' (龍角寺岩屋古墳) is a square Kofun (方墳) located in {{ill|Sakae|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae|qq=}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is designated as No. 105 in the [[:en:Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster]], which comprises 115 Kofuns. Together with the Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, it is recognized as a [[Historic Site of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:100501 R Iwaya kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Aerial view of Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (2010) from the Geospatial Information Authority of Japan aerial photograph]] The kofun is located on a terrace about 30 meters above sea level, on the northern edge of {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}, a marsh in Chiba Prefecture. Scholars estimate its construction to the early–mid 7th century, during the late Kofun period.Kurita (2005), p. 158; Shiraishi (2007), p. 178; Ozawa (2008), p. 336. However, the lack of unearthed artifacts from the site has led to debates about its precise construction period, with estimations based primarily on the architectural features of the stone chambers.Ōtsuka (2009), p. 39. The neighboring {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} is thought to have been built slightly earlier, around the early 7th century. The mound has three stepped tiers, with each side measuring 78 meters and a height of 13.2 meters. Surrounding it is a 3-meter-wide moat and embankment. With its large size, the kofun surpasses contemporary square mounds such as the {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Emperor Yōmei) and {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Empress Suiko). Nationally, it Ranks as the second-largest square Kofun after {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}}, built in the early 5th century in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]].Shiraishi (2007), p. 159. The southern face of the mound features two horizontal stone chambers positioned 10 meters apart. The western chamber measures 4.23 meters in length, with a rear wall width of 1.68 meters and a height of 2.14 meters. The eastern chamber, slightly larger, has partially collapsed. Both chambers were constructed with sandstone from the {{ill|Kinoshita Formation|ja|下総層群木下層|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita Formation|qq=}}, known for its abundant fossilized shells.[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n431-019.html Kinoshita Formation] (Chiba Prefecture). File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Southwest corner of the mound (stone chamber opening visible in the background) File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Northwest corner and embankment File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the eastern and western chambers File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance to the eastern chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-4.jpg|Entrance to the western chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Interior of the western chamber == Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster == Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun is part of the larger Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, which contains 114 known Kofuns. Located on the [[:en:Shimōsa Plateau|Shimōsa Plateau]] in northeastern Chiba, construction in the cluster is believed to have started in the 6th century with smaller [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and round Kofuns (円墳). By the early 7th century, larger keyhole-shaped mounds such as the Sengenyama Kofun were built, followed by square mounds like the Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun. == Design and Burial Facilities == The mound measures 78 meters per side and rises 13.2 meters high. It features three stepped tiers with a moat surrounding three sides and an embankment beyond. In 2008, surveyors identified a ramp on the southern side leading to the mound.Jinno (2009), p. 15. Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from [[:en:Mount Tsukuba|Mount Tsukuba]] was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakae, Chiba]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[en:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:千葉県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:千葉県の古墳]]

In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakae, Chiba]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[en:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:千葉県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:栄町の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → National historic sites in Chiba prefecture
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site of Japan |name=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (龍角寺岩屋古墳) |image=[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance |location={{ill|Sakae, Chiba|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae, Chiba|qq=}}, Chiba Prefecture, Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|49|16|N|140|16|38|E|region:JP-12_scale:20000}} |shape=Square Kofun |dimensions=78 m on each side, 13.2 m in height |construction_period=Early–mid 7th century |burial goods=Haniwa in the shape of waterfowl |designation=National Historic Site (1941) |Burial Facility=Two horizontal stone chambers |map={{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=49|lat_sec=16|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=38|label=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Location in Chiba Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun''' (龍角寺岩屋古墳) is a square Kofun (方墳) located in {{ill|Sakae|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae|qq=}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is designated as No. 105 in the [[:en:Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster]], which comprises 115 Kofuns. Together with the Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, it is recognized as a [[Historic Site of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:100501 R Iwaya kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Aerial view of Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (2010) from the Geospatial Information Authority of Japan aerial photograph]] The kofun is located on a terrace about 30 meters above sea level, on the northern edge of {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}, a marsh in Chiba Prefecture. Scholars estimate its construction to the early–mid 7th century, during the late Kofun period.Kurita (2005), p. 158; Shiraishi (2007), p. 178; Ozawa (2008), p. 336. However, the lack of unearthed artifacts from the site has led to debates about its precise construction period, with estimations based primarily on the architectural features of the stone chambers.Ōtsuka (2009), p. 39. The neighboring {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} is thought to have been built slightly earlier, around the early 7th century. The mound has three stepped tiers, with each side measuring 78 meters and a height of 13.2 meters. Surrounding it is a 3-meter-wide moat and embankment. With its large size, the kofun surpasses contemporary square mounds such as the {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Emperor Yōmei) and {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Empress Suiko). Nationally, it Ranks as the second-largest square Kofun after {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}}, built in the early 5th century in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]].Shiraishi (2007), p. 159. The southern face of the mound features two horizontal stone chambers positioned 10 meters apart. The western chamber measures 4.23 meters in length, with a rear wall width of 1.68 meters and a height of 2.14 meters. The eastern chamber, slightly larger, has partially collapsed. Both chambers were constructed with sandstone from the {{ill|Kinoshita Formation|ja|下総層群木下層|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita Formation|qq=}}, known for its abundant fossilized shells.[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n431-019.html Kinoshita Formation] (Chiba Prefecture). File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Southwest corner of the mound (stone chamber opening visible in the background) File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Northwest corner and embankment File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the eastern and western chambers File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance to the eastern chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-4.jpg|Entrance to the western chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Interior of the western chamber == Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster == Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun is part of the larger Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, which contains 114 known Kofuns. Located on the [[:en:Shimōsa Plateau|Shimōsa Plateau]] in northeastern Chiba, construction in the cluster is believed to have started in the 6th century with smaller [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and round Kofuns (円墳). By the early 7th century, larger keyhole-shaped mounds such as the Sengenyama Kofun were built, followed by square mounds like the Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun. == Design and Burial Facilities == The mound measures 78 meters per side and rises 13.2 meters high. It features three stepped tiers with a moat surrounding three sides and an embankment beyond. In 2008, surveyors identified a ramp on the southern side leading to the mound.Jinno (2009), p. 15. Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from [[:en:Mount Tsukuba|Mount Tsukuba]] was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakae, Chiba]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[en:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:National historic sites in Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:栄町の歴史]]

Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from [[:en:Mount Tsukuba|Mount Tsukuba]] was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakae, Chiba]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:National historic sites in Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:栄町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}}

{{Infobox ancient site of Japan |name=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (龍角寺岩屋古墳) |image=[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance |location={{ill|Sakae, Chiba|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae, Chiba|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Chiba Prefecture, Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|49|16|N|140|16|38|E|region:JP-12_scale:20000}} |shape=Square Kofun |dimensions=78 m on each side, 13.2 m in height |construction_period=Early–mid 7th century |burial goods=Haniwa in the shape of waterfowl |designation=National Historic Site (1941) |Burial Facility=Two horizontal stone chambers |map={{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=49|lat_sec=16|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=38|label=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Location in Chiba Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun''' (龍角寺岩屋古墳) is a square Kofun (方墳) located in {{ill|Sakae|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is designated as No. 105 in the [[:en:Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster]], which comprises 115 Kofuns. Together with the Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, it is recognized as a [[Historic Site of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:100501 R Iwaya kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Aerial view of Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (2010) from the Geospatial Information Authority of Japan aerial photograph]] The kofun is located on a terrace about 30 meters above sea level, on the northern edge of {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}, a marsh in Chiba Prefecture. Scholars estimate its construction to the early–mid 7th century, during the late Kofun period.Kurita (2005), p. 158; Shiraishi (2007), p. 178; Ozawa (2008), p. 336. However, the lack of unearthed artifacts from the site has led to debates about its precise construction period, with estimations based primarily on the architectural features of the stone chambers.Ōtsuka (2009), p. 39. The neighboring {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} is thought to have been built slightly earlier, around the early 7th century. The mound has three stepped tiers, with each side measuring 78 meters and a height of 13.2 meters. Surrounding it is a 3-meter-wide moat and embankment. With its large size, the kofun surpasses contemporary square mounds such as the {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Emperor Yōmei) and {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Empress Suiko). Nationally, it Ranks as the second-largest square Kofun after {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}}, built in the early 5th century in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]].Shiraishi (2007), p. 159. The southern face of the mound features two horizontal stone chambers positioned 10 meters apart. The western chamber measures 4.23 meters in length, with a rear wall width of 1.68 meters and a height of 2.14 meters. The eastern chamber, slightly larger, has partially collapsed. Both chambers were constructed with sandstone from the {{ill|Kinoshita Formation|ja|下総層群木下層|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita Formation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, known for its abundant fossilized shells.[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n431-019.html Kinoshita Formation] (Chiba Prefecture). File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Southwest corner of the mound (stone chamber opening visible in the background) File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Northwest corner and embankment File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the eastern and western chambers File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance to the eastern chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-4.jpg|Entrance to the western chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Interior of the western chamber == Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster == Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun is part of the larger Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, which contains 114 known Kofuns. Located on the [[:en:Shimōsa Plateau|Shimōsa Plateau]] in northeastern Chiba, construction in the cluster is believed to have started in the 6th century with smaller [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and round Kofuns (円墳). By the early 7th century, larger keyhole-shaped mounds such as the Sengenyama Kofun were built, followed by square mounds like the Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun. == Design and Burial Facilities == The mound measures 78 meters per side and rises 13.2 meters high. It features three stepped tiers with a moat surrounding three sides and an embankment beyond. In 2008, surveyors identified a ramp on the southern side leading to the mound.Jinno (2009), p. 15. Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from [[:en:Mount Tsukuba|Mount Tsukuba]] was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakae, Chiba]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:National historic sites in Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:栄町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}}

Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:National Historic Sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:National historic sites in Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:栄町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site of Japan |name=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (龍角寺岩屋古墳) |image=[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance |location={{ill|Sakae, Chiba|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae, Chiba|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Chiba Prefecture, Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|49|16|N|140|16|38|E|region:JP-12_scale:20000}} |shape=Square Kofun |dimensions=78 m on each side, 13.2 m in height |construction_period=Early–mid 7th century |burial goods=Haniwa in the shape of waterfowl |designation=National Historic Site (1941) |Burial Facility=Two horizontal stone chambers |map={{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=49|lat_sec=16|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=38|label=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Location in Chiba Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun''' (龍角寺岩屋古墳) is a square Kofun (方墳) located in {{ill|Sakae|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is designated as No. 105 in the [[:en:Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster]], which comprises 115 Kofuns. Together with the Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, it is recognized as a [[Historic Site of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:100501 R Iwaya kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Aerial view of Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (2010) from the Geospatial Information Authority of Japan aerial photograph]] The kofun is located on a terrace about 30 meters above sea level, on the northern edge of {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}, a marsh in Chiba Prefecture. Scholars estimate its construction to the early–mid 7th century, during the late Kofun period.Kurita (2005), p. 158; Shiraishi (2007), p. 178; Ozawa (2008), p. 336. However, the lack of unearthed artifacts from the site has led to debates about its precise construction period, with estimations based primarily on the architectural features of the stone chambers.Ōtsuka (2009), p. 39. The neighboring {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} is thought to have been built slightly earlier, around the early 7th century. The mound has three stepped tiers, with each side measuring 78 meters and a height of 13.2 meters. Surrounding it is a 3-meter-wide moat and embankment. With its large size, the kofun surpasses contemporary square mounds such as the {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Emperor Yōmei) and {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Empress Suiko). Nationally, it Ranks as the second-largest square Kofun after {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}}, built in the early 5th century in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]].Shiraishi (2007), p. 159. The southern face of the mound features two horizontal stone chambers positioned 10 meters apart. The western chamber measures 4.23 meters in length, with a rear wall width of 1.68 meters and a height of 2.14 meters. The eastern chamber, slightly larger, has partially collapsed. Both chambers were constructed with sandstone from the {{ill|Kinoshita Formation|ja|下総層群木下層|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita Formation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, known for its abundant fossilized shells.[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n431-019.html Kinoshita Formation] (Chiba Prefecture). File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Southwest corner of the mound (stone chamber opening visible in the background) File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Northwest corner and embankment File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the eastern and western chambers File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance to the eastern chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-4.jpg|Entrance to the western chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Interior of the western chamber == Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster == Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun is part of the larger Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, which contains 114 known Kofuns. Located on the [[:en:Shimōsa Plateau|Shimōsa Plateau]] in northeastern Chiba, construction in the cluster is believed to have started in the 6th century with smaller [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and round Kofuns (円墳). By the early 7th century, larger keyhole-shaped mounds such as the Sengenyama Kofun were built, followed by square mounds like the Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun. == Design and Burial Facilities == The mound measures 78 meters per side and rises 13.2 meters high. It features three stepped tiers with a moat surrounding three sides and an embankment beyond. In 2008, surveyors identified a ramp on the southern side leading to the mound.Jinno (2009), p. 15. Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from [[:en:Mount Tsukuba|Mount Tsukuba]] was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:栄町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}}

Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from [[:en:Mount Tsukuba|Mount Tsukuba]] was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sakae (Chiba)]] {{Commons category|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site of Japan |name=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (龍角寺岩屋古墳) |image=[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance |location={{ill|Sakae, Chiba|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae, Chiba|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Chiba Prefecture, Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|49|16|N|140|16|38|E|region:JP-12_scale:20000}} |shape=Square Kofun |dimensions=78 m on each side, 13.2 m in height |construction_period=Early–mid 7th century |burial goods=Haniwa in the shape of waterfowl |designation=National Historic Site (1941) |Burial Facility=Two horizontal stone chambers |map={{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=49|lat_sec=16|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=38|label=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Location in Chiba Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun''' (龍角寺岩屋古墳) is a square Kofun (方墳) located in {{ill|Sakae|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is designated as No. 105 in the [[:en:Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster]], which comprises 115 Kofuns. Together with the Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, it is recognized as a [[Historic Site of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:100501 R Iwaya kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Aerial view of Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (2010) from the Geospatial Information Authority of Japan aerial photograph]] The kofun is located on a terrace about 30 meters above sea level, on the northern edge of {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}, a marsh in Chiba Prefecture. Scholars estimate its construction to the early–mid 7th century, during the late Kofun period.Kurita (2005), p. 158; Shiraishi (2007), p. 178; Ozawa (2008), p. 336. However, the lack of unearthed artifacts from the site has led to debates about its precise construction period, with estimations based primarily on the architectural features of the stone chambers.Ōtsuka (2009), p. 39. The neighboring {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} is thought to have been built slightly earlier, around the early 7th century. The mound has three stepped tiers, with each side measuring 78 meters and a height of 13.2 meters. Surrounding it is a 3-meter-wide moat and embankment. With its large size, the kofun surpasses contemporary square mounds such as the {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Emperor Yōmei) and {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Empress Suiko). Nationally, it Ranks as the second-largest square Kofun after {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}}, built in the early 5th century in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]].Shiraishi (2007), p. 159. The southern face of the mound features two horizontal stone chambers positioned 10 meters apart. The western chamber measures 4.23 meters in length, with a rear wall width of 1.68 meters and a height of 2.14 meters. The eastern chamber, slightly larger, has partially collapsed. Both chambers were constructed with sandstone from the {{ill|Kinoshita Formation|ja|下総層群木下層|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita Formation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, known for its abundant fossilized shells.[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n431-019.html Kinoshita Formation] (Chiba Prefecture). File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Southwest corner of the mound (stone chamber opening visible in the background) File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Northwest corner and embankment File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the eastern and western chambers File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance to the eastern chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-4.jpg|Entrance to the western chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Interior of the western chamber == Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster == Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun is part of the larger Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, which contains 114 known Kofuns. Located on the [[:en:Shimōsa Plateau|Shimōsa Plateau]] in northeastern Chiba, construction in the cluster is believed to have started in the 6th century with smaller [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and round Kofuns (円墳). By the early 7th century, larger keyhole-shaped mounds such as the Sengenyama Kofun were built, followed by square mounds like the Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun. == Design and Burial Facilities == The mound measures 78 meters per side and rises 13.2 meters high. It features three stepped tiers with a moat surrounding three sides and an embankment beyond. In 2008, surveyors identified a ramp on the southern side leading to the mound.Jinno (2009), p. 15. Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from [[:en:Mount Tsukuba|Mount Tsukuba]] was used for platforms supporting coffins.Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167. == Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education. == Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site]. == Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]] == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/643 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun - National Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.town.sakae.chiba.jp/index.php?code=874 Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster List - Sakae Town] ==References== {{translated page|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|version=96035321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[ja:龍角寺岩屋古墳]] [[sn:yūkakuji Iwaya Kofun]] [[Category:Chiba Heritage Top 100]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sakae (Chiba)]] {{Commons category|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}}

File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Southwest corner of the mound (stone chamber opening visible in the background) File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Northwest corner and embankment File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the eastern and western chambers File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance to the eastern chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-4.jpg|Entrance to the western chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Interior of the western chamber == Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster == Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun is part of the larger Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, which contains 114 known Kofuns. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11679372]] Wikidata name: Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site of Japan |name=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (龍角寺岩屋古墳) |image=File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun.jpg |location={{ill|Sakae|qid=Q1202754}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.821111|N|140.277222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=Square Kofun |dimensions=78 m on each side, 13.2 m in height |construction_period=Early–mid 7th century |burial goods=Haniwa in the shape of waterfowl |designation=National Historic Site (1941) |Burial Facility=Two horizontal stone chambers |map={{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=49|lat_sec=16|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=38|label=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Location in Chiba Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |architectural_styles={{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353}} }}
'''Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun''' (龍角寺岩屋古墳) is a square Kofun (方墳) located in {{ill|Sakae|ja|栄町 (千葉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is designated as No. 105 in the [[:en:Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster]], which comprises 115 Kofuns. Together with the Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, it is recognized as a [[Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview ==
[[File:100501 R Iwaya kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Aerial view of Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun (2010) from the Geospatial Information Authority of Japan aerial photograph]] The kofun is located on a terrace about 30 meters above sea level, on the northern edge of {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}, a marsh in Chiba Prefecture. Scholars estimate its construction to the early–mid 7th century, during the late Kofun period.<ref>Kurita (2005), p. 158; Shiraishi (2007), p. 178; Ozawa (2008), p. 336.</ref> However, the lack of unearthed artifacts from the site has led to debates about its precise construction period, with estimations based primarily on the architectural features of the stone chambers.<ref>Ōtsuka (2009), p. 39.</ref> The neighboring {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} is thought to have been built slightly earlier, around the early 7th century.
The mound has three stepped tiers, with each side measuring 78 meters and a height of 13.2 meters. Surrounding it is a 3-meter-wide moat and embankment. With its large size, the kofun surpasses contemporary square mounds such as the {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Emperor Yōmei) and {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (tomb of Empress Suiko). Nationally, it Ranks as the second-largest square Kofun after {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}}, built in the early 5th century in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]].<ref>Shiraishi (2007), p. 159.</ref>
The southern face of the mound features two horizontal stone chambers positioned 10 meters apart. The western chamber measures 4.23 meters in length, with a rear wall width of 1.68 meters and a height of 2.14 meters. The eastern chamber, slightly larger, has partially collapsed. Both chambers were constructed with sandstone from the {{ill|Kinoshita Formation|ja|下総層群木下層|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita Formation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, known for its abundant fossilized shells.<ref>[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n431-019.html Kinoshita Formation] (Chiba Prefecture).</ref>
<gallery> File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Southwest corner of the mound (stone chamber opening visible in the background) File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Northwest corner and embankment File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the eastern and western chambers File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance to the eastern chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, entrance-4.jpg|Entrance to the western chamber File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|Interior of the western chamber </gallery>
== Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster == Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun is part of the larger Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster, which contains 114 known Kofuns. Located on the [[:en:Shimōsa Plateau|Shimōsa Plateau]] in northeastern Chiba, construction in the cluster is believed to have started in the 6th century with smaller [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and round Kofuns (円墳). By the early 7th century, larger keyhole-shaped mounds such as the Sengenyama Kofun were built, followed by square mounds like the Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun.
== Design and Burial Facilities == The mound measures 78 meters per side and rises 13.2 meters high. It features three stepped tiers with a moat surrounding three sides and an embankment beyond. In 2008, surveyors identified a ramp on the southern side leading to the mound.<ref>Jinno (2009), p. 15.</ref>
Both horizontal stone chambers lack passageways, consisting only of burial chambers. The chambers were built using sandstone from the Kinoshita Formation, while schist from [[:en:Mount Tsukuba|Mount Tsukuba]] was used for platforms supporting coffins.<ref>Kurita (2005), p. 159; Shiraishi (2007), pp. 166–167.</ref>
== Timeline == * 1970: Survey by Meiji University to map the mound and chambers. * 2007: Cleaning and survey of Mound 104 by the Sakae Board of Education.
== Historic Site Designation == The site was designated a National Historic Site on January 27, 1941. In 2009, the designation was expanded to include surrounding areas and renamed "Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster and Iwaya Kofun."<ref>[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/politics_economy_kiji.php?i=nesp1227317452 Chiba Nippo - Ryukakuji Kofun Cluster designated a National Historic Site].</ref>
== Address == 1601 Ikeshita, Ryukakuji, Sakae, Chiba Prefecture, Japan == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]] * [[List of Historic Sites in Kanto]]
